prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock price volatility patterns, assuming an annualized volatility of approximately 25-30% for individual stocks. For an 18-day forecast horizon, this translates to roughly 4-5% daily standard deviation scaled to the time period. The point estimate assumes minimal drift with slight upward bias. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions with increasing width for higher confidence levels, accounting for the uncertainty that grows with the forecast horizon.
